---
title: "“Thinkering” with Digital Research Infrastructures for Open Science in Humanities"
subtitle: "Infrastructures in the Making: Local Experiments and Systemic Change in Open Science"
authors: 
  - name: Tugce Karatas
    orcid: 0000-0002-9446-4679
    affiliations: Luxembourg Centre for Contemporary and Digital History (C²DH)
  - name: Elisabeth Guerard
    orcid: 0000-0001-7742-4141
    affiliations: Luxembourg Centre for Contemporary and Digital History (C²DH)
lang: fr
editor: source
format:
  revealjs: 
    slide-number: true
    # template-partials:
    #  - title-slide.html
    theme: [default]
    logo: img/logo-operas.png
    header-logo: img/logo-operas.png
#    hide-from-titleSlide: text
    footer: "OPERAS 2026 - 21 May 2026"
    scrollable: TRUE
    smaller: TRUE
    overview: TRUE
    chalkboard: TRUE
#    transition: fade
    transition-speed: fast
    bibliography: biblio.bib
    menu: TRUE
    css: style.css
    # pointer:
    #  pointerSize: 20
    #  color: 'red4'
#krevealjs-plugins:
#  - pointer
# filters:
#  - reveal-header
engine: markdown
---

# Open science and digital infrastructures

- Who are we?
- How we work?
- General view
Open science no national guidelines - no national repository


::: notes 

:::

# C2DH and DRI

- DRI people
- DRI toolbox
GitLab, Nainuwa, Dataverse, Nodegoat, CatDV, Orbilu, Transkribus


# The challenge

see Abstract
- Quality of the datas 
- Legal requirements
- Preservation
- No common solution

# “Thinkering”

- To the beginning of the project to the end
- In house / off-the-shelf

# Cost and benefit

First trial with commercial solution

# Conclusion

- Pression to innovate
- Open science required more time

# What we need

Intermediate place - try to find solution IN-BETWEEN

Use feedback of the user

> MISSION to comply with the open science

# Bibliography
